checks.finding: All six in-scope requirements resolve to concrete, measurable assertions: REQ-001 pins exact exit codes and terminal strings (AC-001/AC-002), a documented state-machine rule with file:line citation (AC-003), a grep-based self-check (AC-004); REQ-002 pins seed content categories and exit-code behavior (AC-007..009); REQ-003/REQ-004 pin parameter surface, precondition parity, and ASCII/no-BOM/no-CR byte checks (AC-011..016); REQ-005 pins twin existence plus a decreasing named-SKIP count on windows-latest CI (AC-013/AC-016/AC-017); REQ-006 pins validate-repository/skill-reference-sync green plus a CHANGELOG entry (AC-019). No in-scope requirement lacks an observable validation path.

Every REQ-001..REQ-006 goal is explicitly tagged in acceptance-tests.md's table: REQ-001->AC-001..006, REQ-002->AC-007..010, REQ-003->AC-011..013, REQ-004->AC-014..016, REQ-005 spans AC-006/010/013/016/017, REQ-006->AC-019. No orphan goal and no AC without a REQ back-reference.

AC-001..AC-019 each describe externally observable artifacts or state: exact exit codes and printed strings (AC-001/002/008/009/014), byte-level hygiene checks (AC-012/015), a named-SKIP-to-green CI transition recorded in the implementation report (AC-013/016), and file/directory existence with verbatim content (AC-007). None rely on unobservable internal intent.

requirements.md's Non-goals section explicitly excludes re-registering the loop inventory, driving wfi-audit-cycle end-to-end via an LLM agent or exercising gh, re-litigating issue #127, extending downstream-review-precheck-parity to spec/domain, touching any protected-gate file (with citation to _PROTECTED_GATE_SUFFIXES, sdd-hook-guard.py:886-927), tasks.md/traceability.md (Phase 2), and Pillar B/#125. The boundary between this feature and adjacent Pillar-A/B work is explicit.

Security Boundaries table (B1-B4: fixture/real-tree isolation, real-gate-driven-not-reimplemented, hook-guard command-line exposure, GitHub non-reachability) plus Edge Cases (bash-3.2 array expansion INV-029, pwd -P TMPDIR normalization INV-030, Windows jq CRLF stripping INV-031, real-validator Windows-CRLF fragility gated through loop_validator_capability_probe/loop_validator_skip INV-032) plus Roles/Permissions (human maintainer approval boundary for specs/tasks) together state every material constraint implied by the requirements, each with file:line/INV citation.

The Risks section maps each of 4 identified risks (Critical: skill-drift reimplementation risk; High: improperly-wired CHECK stub false-positive; Medium x2: shared-file edit collision, brownfield leg regression) to a named mitigation citing a specific AC (AC-005's real-WFI-document smoke check, AC-002's mandatory reproduces-on-iteration-3 canary, AC-010's positive non-SKIP assertion). Every high-risk claim has a planned validation surface; SKIP was not applicable since risk claims are present.
